findingVerification.notes: The benchmark warden.toml explicitly sets defaults.verification.enabled = true.

traceCapture.notes: Run used --traces on all shards after runtime child-span trace capture was fixed. Per-chunk trace metadata is preserved in traceSummaries and the raw JSONL artifacts.

notes: Full benchmark result stitched from one Warden run per corpus SHA using --runtime pi --model anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 --effort high --traces. Each shard analyzes every unique corpus path for that SHA, not the whole Sentry repository. Raw JSONL artifacts are withheld pending sensitive-data review; committed traceSummaries retain sanitized per-chunk metadata only.

scoring.notes: Agent-verified semantic matches. A finding counts when it identifies the same bug in roughly the same location as an existing corpus finding. Same-file findings about different bugs do not count.

scores.notes: Same InternalEAFeaturesEndpoint object-permission bypass.
Same OAuth userinfo inactive-user/application validation gap; does not cover the hashed-token corpus issue.
Same system options allowlist tuple bug.
Same GitHub Actions output injection through newline-capable PR filenames.
Same unsigned Bitbucket Server webhook exposure.
Same pull-request size-analysis download project-access bypass.
Timing-unsafe flag secret comparison is not the Statsig timestamp freshness corpus issue in this file.
Same project size-analysis comparison download project-access bypass.
Same uuid1 release webhook token weakness.
Same relocation retry existence/status disclosure before authorization.
Same org-level Seer RPC project-access bypass.
Rate-limit gap is not one of the known corpus issues for organization_seer_rpc.py.
Same Jira JQL autocomplete backslash escaping bug.
Same feature flag ECharts tooltip HTML injection.
Same Codecov repository token regeneration scope bug.
Same saved-query visit endpoint object-permission bug in Explore and Discover.
Same external team lookup scoped by organization instead of team.
Same MS Teams action handler group/integration organization binding bug.
Same public preprod size-analysis project-access bypass. The grouped related locations were not separately counted.
Same Perforce password exposure via configData.
Same GCP transfer job PubSub webhook authentication gap.
Perforce install SSRF is not the Perforce credential-exposure corpus issue.
Same Seer autofix run_id/group binding bug.
Same-file system options finding in fbecd17d, but the corpus issue for that SHA is cleartext option-value logging.
